title: old() looks like a function call but is a compiler keyword — callers will misuse it
description: old(expr) in ensures clauses looks like a regular function call. It isn't — it is a compiler-magic keyword that captures a value at function entry. Callers who try to use old() outside an ensures clause (in a requires clause, in a regular expression, in a helper function) will get a confusing error. More importantly, callers reading the signature will not know old() is special until they hit the error. A dedicated syntax — old\!expr, #old(expr), or @old(expr) using Zig's builtin convention — would signal that this is not a user-space call.
